Status: In Progress with PR Approval Delays
Risk_Title: Recurring PR Approval Delays Impacting Sprint Velocity
Risk_Description: Throughout all 8 sprints, bug fixes and enhancements consistently faced PR approval delays causing cycle time increases from 3-4 days to 5-6 days. This pattern affected sprint velocity (40-42 tasks/sprint) and caused minor timeline slippages. The delay cascaded to QA retesting cycles, increasing overall delivery time and resource utilization.
Tech_Stack: Git, CI/CD Pipeline, Code Review Tools
Potential_Impact: Delayed feature delivery, increased cycle time, resource idle time, potential SLA misses

Status: In Testing with Multiple Retest Cycles
SLA_Metric: QA Pass Rate: 92% → 96% (Improving but with high retest cycles)
Risk_Title: High QA Retesting Cycles Causing Budget Overrun
Risk_Description: QA team identified defects and performed 2-3 retesting cycles per defect throughout all sprints. Some defects were reopened due to incomplete fixes (Reopen Count: 0-2 per task). This pattern caused QA budget overrun of ₹25,000 (8.3% over budget) and extended testing timelines. The high retesting cycle indicates quality issues in initial development and incomplete defect resolution.
Tech_Stack: QA Testing, Selenium, JIRA, Test Management Tools
Potential_Impact: Budget overrun, extended testing timelines, delayed go-live, reduced software quality confidence

SLA_Metric: Dependency delays: 3-5 blocked tasks per sprint
Risk_Title: Environment Access and Dependency Delays Blocking Progress
Risk_Description: Dependency tracking tasks were consistently blocked due to delays in UAT/prod access permissions, environment readiness, and external approvals. This pattern occurred across all 8 sprints with 3-5 blocked tasks per sprint. The delays impacted integration testing, production monitoring setup, and overall project timeline. Follow-ups were required repeatedly for access permissions causing operational inefficiencies.
Potential_Impact: Blocked tasks, delayed integration testing, production readiness delays, team idle time

Risk_Title: Production Monitoring Increase Causing DevOps Budget Overrun
Risk_Description: Integration validation, data flow checks, production monitoring, and log analysis tasks faced delays due to environment access and external dependencies. Production monitoring workload increased significantly causing DevOps budget overrun of ₹20,000 (10% over budget). The pattern shows infrastructure dependency issues and increased operational overhead for production support activities.
Tech_Stack: DevOps, Monitoring Tools, Log Analysis, Infrastructure
Potential_Impact: Budget overrun, increased operational costs, production stability risks, delayed issue resolution

Status: Controlled at 3-4% but with Medium Leakage
Risk_Title: Defect Leakage Risk Despite QA Retesting Cycles
Risk_Description: Despite multiple QA retesting cycles and improving QA pass rate (92% → 96%), defect leakage remained at 3-4% throughout the project. This indicates quality gaps in initial development and incomplete defect resolution. The medium defect leakage level poses risks for production stability and customer satisfaction. The pattern suggests need for enhanced development quality practices and more thorough initial testing.
Tech_Stack: QA Testing, Defect Management, Quality Assurance
Potential_Impact: Production defects, customer dissatisfaction, increased support costs, reputation damage

Risk_Title: Developer Resource Utilization Spike Due to Fixes and Rework
Risk_Description: Developer resource utilization spiked from planned 70% to actual 82% due to higher workload from bug fixes, PR rework, and QA support. This 12% increase indicates resource strain and potential burnout risk. The pattern shows that initial development quality issues led to increased rework and support activities, consuming more resources than planned.
Potential_Impact: Team burnout, quality degradation, attrition risk, missed deadlines, reduced morale

Risk_Title: QA Automation Coverage Gap Requiring Mid-Project Resource Addition
Risk_Description: Divya was added mid-project in Sprint 4 to improve QA automation coverage and defect revalidation. This indicates initial QA automation gaps and insufficient regression testing coverage. The addition helped improve QA pass rate from 92% to 96% and reduced regression turnaround time, but also indicates initial planning gaps in QA resource allocation and automation strategy.
Tech_Stack: QA Automation, Selenium, Test Automation Framework
Potential_Impact: Delayed automation, increased manual testing costs, regression gaps, quality risks

Status: In Testing with Certificate and Access Delays
SLA_Metric: Certificate renewal and production log access blocked
Risk_Title: Certificate Renewal and Production Access Delays Blocking Performance Testing
Risk_Description: Performance testing and data reconciliation activities in Sprint 5-6 were blocked by certificate renewal delays and production log access issues. This pattern shows infrastructure and security process gaps that delayed critical performance validation activities. The delays posed risks for production readiness and go-live timeline.
Potential_Impact: Delayed performance testing, production readiness risks, go-live delays, security vulnerabilities

Status: Blocked by Client UAT Signoff and Defect Retest Queue
Risk_Title: Client UAT Sign-off Delays and Defect Retest Queue Blocking Go-Live
Risk_Description: UAT support activities in Sprint 6-7 were blocked by client UAT signoff delays and defect retest queue buildup. This pattern shows client engagement gaps and defect resolution bottlenecks that delayed final acceptance and go-live. The delays posed risks for project timeline and budget overruns if extended beyond planned 22-week timeline.
Potential_Impact: Go-live delays, budget overruns, client dissatisfaction, contract penalties

SLA_Metric: Zero data drop incidents for 72 consecutive hours target
Risk_Title: Cutover Checklist Approval and Interface Freeze Delays Blocking Go-Live
Risk_Description: Cutover rehearsal and production monitoring activities in Sprint 7-8 were blocked by cutover checklist approval and interface freeze confirmation delays. This pattern shows production readiness process gaps and approval bottlenecks that delayed final go-live. The delays posed risks for production stability and successful cutover execution.
Potential_Impact: Go-live delays, production stability risks, data loss risks, rollback requirements

Risk_Title: Resource Roll-off Risk During Critical Hypercare Period
Risk_Description: Bala completed hypercare handover and rolled off after Sprint 8, during the critical 30-day hypercare period. This pattern shows resource transition risks during production stabilization phase. The roll-off poses knowledge transfer gaps and support coverage risks during the most critical post-go-live period when production issues are most likely to occur.
Potential_Impact: Knowledge loss, support gaps, production incident delays, customer dissatisfaction

Risk_Title: EMPI Patient Matching Accuracy Risk Below Threshold
Risk_Description: The project requires Enterprise Master Patient Index (EMPI) patient matching accuracy of ≥99.5%. Failure to achieve this threshold poses significant risks for patient safety, data integrity, and regulatory compliance (HIPAA, HITECH). The probabilistic matching algorithm must be extensively tested and tuned to meet this critical requirement. Any accuracy below threshold requires immediate investigation and algorithm tuning.
Potential_Impact: Patient safety risks, data integrity failures, regulatory violations, legal liability, reputation damage

SLA_Metric: API Response Time: ≤3 seconds (95th percentile) target
Risk_Title: API Performance Degradation Risk Impacting Clinical Workflows
Risk_Description: The project requires API response time of ≤3 seconds for 95% of queries to ensure clinicians can access longitudinal patient records quickly. Performance degradation poses risks for clinical workflow efficiency and user adoption. Load testing in pre-production and performance monitoring are critical to identify and resolve performance bottlenecks before go-live.
Tech_Stack: FHIR API, HL7 Integration, Performance Optimization
Potential_Impact: Clinical workflow delays, user dissatisfaction, system abandonment, patient care delays

Status: Target: Zero discrepancies in allergies and medications
Risk_Title: Data Integrity Risk in Allergy and Medication Mapping Across Systems
Risk_Description: The project requires zero discrepancies in allergies and medications mapped across systems (Epic, Athenahealth, State Registry). Data integrity failures pose critical patient safety risks and regulatory compliance violations. Clinical validation with sample of 50 patient records is required to ensure accurate data mapping and terminology standardization (LOINC, RxNorm).
Potential_Impact: Patient safety risks, medication errors, allergic reactions, legal liability, regulatory violations

Risk_Title: Audit Logging Compliance Risk for HIPAA/HITECH Requirements
Risk_Description: The project requires 100% of data exchanges to be logged with user, patient, and purpose (Treatment, Payment, Operations) for HIPAA/HITECH compliance. Audit logging failures pose regulatory compliance violations and legal liability risks. The audit logging system must capture all access events and consent management activities to meet regulatory requirements.
Potential_Impact: Regulatory violations, legal penalties, audit failures, reputation damage, data breach risks

Risk_Title: Consent Management Propagation Delays Violating Patient Privacy Rights
Risk_Description: The project requires patient opt-out directives to propagate to all connected systems within 24 hours to protect patient privacy rights. Consent management delays pose regulatory compliance violations (HIPAA, state privacy laws) and patient trust issues. The consent management portal must reliably propagate consent changes and audit all consent activities.
Tech_Stack: Consent Management, Patient Privacy, Healthcare Integration
Potential_Impact: Privacy violations, regulatory penalties, patient trust loss, legal liability, reputation damage

Status: Risk: Local codes may not map cleanly to LOINC/RxNorm
Risk_Title: Terminology Mapping Gaps Requiring Manual Curation and Delays
Risk_Description: The project proposal identifies risk that local lab codes may not map cleanly to LOINC and local medication codes may not map to RxNorm, requiring manual curation. This poses risks for data standardization delays, clinical validation failures, and interoperability gaps. Extensive terminology mapping work with clinical SME validation is required to ensure accurate and complete mappings.
Potential_Impact: Data standardization delays, clinical validation failures, interoperability gaps, go-live delays

Status: Risk: AS/400 does not support modern APIs
Risk_Title: Legacy Lab System (AS/400) API Limitations Requiring HL7 v2.5.1 Fallback
Risk_Description: The project proposal identifies that the legacy lab system runs on AS/400 platform which does not support modern APIs, requiring HL7 v2.5.1 fallback instead of FHIR R4. This poses risks for integration complexity, performance limitations, and data transformation challenges. Dedicated legacy system specialist and early integration testing are required to mitigate this risk.
Tech_Stack: AS/400, HL7 v2.5.1, Legacy System Integration
Potential_Impact: Integration complexity, performance limitations, data transformation errors, delayed testing

Status: Risk: Epic and Athenahealth support delays
Risk_Title: Third-Party Vendor (Epic, Athenahealth) Support Delays Blocking Integration
Risk_Description: The project proposal identifies risk that Epic and Athenahealth vendors must provide timely support for interface configuration and testing. Vendor support delays pose risks for integration timeline, testing delays, and go-live delays. Early engagement with vendors, contractual SLAs, and escalation procedures are required to mitigate this dependency risk.
Potential_Impact: Integration delays, testing delays, go-live delays, vendor relationship issues, contract penalties

Task_Item: EHR Version Upgrade Risk During Project
Status: Assumption: No major EHR upgrades during 22-week timeline
Risk_Title: Major EHR Version Upgrade Risk Disrupting Project Timeline and Scope
Risk_Description: The project assumptions explicitly state that no major EHR version upgrades (e.g., Epic Hyperspace migration) will occur during the 22-week project timeline. If this assumption is violated, it poses critical risks for project scope changes, integration rework, testing delays, and timeline extensions. This risk requires active monitoring and escalation if EHR upgrade plans emerge.
Potential_Impact: Major scope changes, integration rework, testing delays, timeline extensions, budget overruns

Risk_Title: Product Owner Availability Gap Causing Decision Delays and Scope Ambiguity
Risk_Description: The project assumptions require dedicated Product Owner availability at 20 hours per week. Insufficient Product Owner availability poses risks for decision delays, scope ambiguity, requirement clarification delays, and stakeholder alignment issues. This risk requires active monitoring of Product Owner engagement and escalation if availability drops below required level.
Potential_Impact: Decision delays, scope ambiguity, requirement gaps, stakeholder misalignment, rework

Risk_Title: Project Health Status Degradation from Green to Amber in Final Weeks
Risk_Description: Weekly Status Reports show project health degraded from Green (Weeks 1-5) to Amber (Weeks 6-8) due to PR approval delays, access dependencies, and production validation issues. This pattern indicates increasing project risks and challenges in final delivery phase. The health degradation requires immediate attention and mitigation actions to prevent further deterioration and ensure successful go-live.
Potential_Impact: Project delays, stakeholder confidence loss, go-live risks, budget overruns, team morale issues

Risk_Title: ROI Monitoring Risk to Ensure Positive Business Value Delivery
Risk_Description: The Finance Report shows positive ROI of 128% and high cost efficiency despite QA and DevOps budget overruns. However, continued budget overruns and timeline delays pose risks for ROI degradation and business value erosion. Active monitoring of ROI metrics and cost efficiency is required to ensure project continues to deliver positive business value and justify investment.
Potential_Impact: ROI degradation, business value erosion, investment justification challenges, stakeholder confidence loss

Risk_Title: TEFCA Compliance Risk for Trusted Exchange Framework Requirements
Risk_Description: The project must align with Trusted Exchange Framework and Common Agreement (TEFCA) requirements for nationwide health information exchange. TEFCA compliance failures pose risks for regulatory violations, interoperability limitations, and legal liability. Continuous monitoring of TEFCA requirements and compliance validation are required throughout the project lifecycle.
Potential_Impact: Regulatory violations, interoperability limitations, legal liability, reputation damage, market access restrictions

Task_Item: Hypercare Support Transition and Knowledge Transfer
Risk_Title: Hypercare Support Transition Risk After 30-Day Period
Risk_Description: The project includes 30-day hypercare support period post-go-live, after which standard support transitions to 8x5 with on-call escalation. The transition from hypercare to standard support poses risks for support coverage gaps, knowledge transfer gaps, and incident response delays. Comprehensive knowledge transfer and support transition planning are required to ensure smooth handover to operations team.
Potential_Impact: Support coverage gaps, knowledge loss, incident response delays, production stability risks, customer dissatisfaction
